question.text: Under Section 15C, what is the penalty for failure to redress investors' grievances?

answer.text: Not less than one lakh rupees, extendable to one lakh per day, maximum one crore rupees
Not less than five lakh rupees, maximum ten crore rupees
Not less than ten lakh rupees with no upper limit

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 15C applies when an intermediary or listed company fails to redress investor grievances after being directed in writing (including electronic communication) by SEBI within a specified period. The penalty is minimum one lakh, up to one lakh per day, maximum one crore. **The Trap:** The penalty is triggered only after SEBI has directed redressal in writing — not automatically for every complaint. The listed company is also covered under this section, not just intermediaries. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The SEBI direction must be in writing (including electronic). Compliance officers should ensure robust grievance redressal systems to avoid triggering this provision.
